key_insight: E^3RL uses the model’s own local cross-entropy as an uncertainty signal to detect and erase faulty reasoning segments, reducing cascading errors in long-horizon generation.
engineering_relevance: The method offers a practical path to more stable, memory-efficient LLM reasoning by enabling self-healing updates while reusing KV cache streams.
method: Derive intrinsic epistemic entropy from endogenous autoregressive cross-entropy.
Apply segment-level adaptive thresholds and advantage allocation to isolate defective logical spans.
Erase erroneous segments and continue reasoning with reused historical KV cache for linear-memory operation.
evidence_signal: Trained on DeepMath-103k and evaluated on mathematical reasoning benchmarks such as AIME, where 4B and 8B models reportedly exceeded prior SOTA by 5.349% and 6.514%.
watch_item: Performance gains depend on reliable segment-level error detection, which may be sensitive to threshold tuning and implementation of cache reuse.
paper_title: Shattering the Autoregressive Curse: Dynamic Epistemic Entropy Orchestrated Erasable Reinforcement Learning for LLMs
